data.title: Tyson closing one of its largest beef processing plants
data.description:
Faced with rising processing costs and shrinking profits in its beef business, Tyson (TSN) is closing its Lexington, Nebraska beef processing plants in an effort designed to “right size its beef business and position it for long-term success.”

According to an announcement by the company, Tyson (TSN) will end operations at the Lexington facility – one of its largest beef processing plants – and convert its Amarillo, Texas facility to a single, full-capacity shift. To meet customer demand, production will be increased at other facilities, optimizing volumes across its network.

The move comes as consumers face soaring beef prices amid an historic shortage of cattle as ranchers deal with high feed prices, drought, and parasite south of the border that led the U.S. Department of Agriculture to restrict the import of cattle from Central and South America.

The impact has driven beef prices up 45% since mid-2024. And although prices are down slightly from the peak of a year ago, the price has steadily increased from July, nearly doubling since the pandemic.

Despite the higher prices, Tyson CEO Donnie King acknowledged that the beef segment is “our only soft spot.”   

“Cattle supplies are at record lows due to drought, potential herd rebuilding and the impact of New World screwworm in Mexico. These factors created market headwinds during the quarter. Despite these challenges, we are strengthening our fundamentals by prioritizing efficiency, reducing cost and introducing innovative products,” King said during the company’s most recent earnings call.

Meanwhile, CFO Curt Calaway forecasted an adjusted loss in the beef segment between $400 million and $600 million with cattle costs for the year ended September up $2B.

At the same time, the Trump Administration is attempting to give some relief to consumers by removing  the 40% tariff on imported beef from Brazil, opening up federal grazing land, and calling on the Department of Justice to investigate potential price fixing in the meat packing industry.

data.title: Captivision.announces strategic transformation and management change
data.description:
• Captivision (NASDAQ:CAPT) announced on Friday its continuing strategic transformation and a resulting management change, as the company is exploring strategic options for Captivision Korea, its wholly owned subsidiary.  
  • The company has accepted the resignation of Anthony Page as CFO.
  • Gary Garrabrant, current CEO of the company, will assume Page's responsibilities on an interim basis, effective immediately.

data.title: Conavi Medical announces public offering of common shares and pre-funded warrants
data.description:
• Conavi Medical  (CNVI:CA) announced on Friday that it has filed a preliminary short form prospectus with the securities regulatory authorities in the Provinces of British Columbia, Alberta and Ontario in connection with an offering of common shares of the company and/or pre-funded common share purchase warrants of the company in lieu of common shares.
  • The company is expected to complete the offering on a commercially reasonable efforts agency basis under an agency agreement that it will enter into with Bloom Burton Securities.
  • The number of securities to be distributed, the size of the offering and the price of each common share and pre-funded warrant will be determined by negotiation between the company and Bloom Burton in the context of the market, with final terms to be determined at the time of pricing.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the Offering to obtain US FDA 510(k) clearance of the next generation Novasight Hybrid system, as well as complete a limited market release and initiate a broader commercial launch in the United States.
  • The company also intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 12, 2025, or such later date as may be agreed upon by the company and Bloom Burton. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Corporación América Airports reports 10.2% rise in October passenger traffic
data.description:
• Corporación América Airports (CAAP) on Friday reported a 10.2% year-on-year (YoY) increase in passenger traffic in October 2025.
  • Domestic passenger traffic rose by 9.1% year over year (YoY), largely driven by Argentina, along with strong performance in Brazil.
  • International traffic grew by 11.6%, with all operating countries contributing positively YoY and particularly strong results in Argentina and Armenia. Notably, Argentina accounted for almost 60% of the total YoY traffic growth in October.
  • In Argentina, total passenger traffic increased by 11.6% YoY, driven by strong performance across both the international and domestic segments.
  • In Italy, passenger traffic grew by 6.8% compared to the same month in 2024. In Brazil, total passenger traffic increased by 9.8% YoY. In Uruguay, total passenger traffic, predominantly international, increased by 6.9% YoY. In Ecuador, passenger traffic increased by 1.2% YoY. In Armenia, passenger traffic increased by a strong 15.3% YoY.
  • Cargo volume increased by 1.1% YoY, with positive contributions from Uruguay, Armenia and Argentina.
  • Aircraft movements increased by 6.9% YoY.
  • CAAP +0.09% after hours to $22.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SNDL renews share repurchase program
data.description:
• SNDL (SNDL) (NASDAQ: SNDL, CSE: SNDL) on Friday said that it has received approval from the Canadian Securities Exchange for the renewal of its share repurchase program, allowing the company to buy back up to C$100 million worth of shares.
  • The program, which caps repurchases at about 24.5 million shares, or 10% of the public float, begins November 21, 2025, and runs through November 20, 2026.
  • SNDL said buybacks may be made through market purchases, private deals, block trades or other permitted methods, and can be suspended or ended at any time.
  • The company repurchased 9.48 million shares under its prior program that ended on November 20, 2025.
  • SNDL +1.86% after hours to $1.64.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. rig count up for fifth time in six weeks in latest Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. jumped by 5 to 554, the rig count's fifth gain in six weeks but still down 29 rigs, or 5%, from last year at this time, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest weekly report.

In the week ending November 21, drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. rose by 2 to 419 and gas rigs gained 2 to 127, while 8 rigs were classified as miscellaneous.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin added 1 to 254, and the Williston Basin and Eagle Ford counts remained unchanged at 29 and 28, respectively.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: Merck gets FDA nod for Keytruda and Padcev combo in bladder cancer treatment
data.description:
• Merck (MRK) announced that the FDA has granted approval for Keytruda and Keytruda Qlex to be used together with Padcev as an added treatment for bladder cancer. 
  • This combination is intended for patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer, first as a neoadjuvant treatment before surgery and then as an adjuvant treatment following cystectomy.
  • The FDA's decision is supported by findings from the phase 3 KEYNOTE-905 trial, which was carried out in partnership with Pfizer and Astellas., which markets Padcev.

data.title: SA Asks: Has the crypto market bottomed yet, or will it go lower?
data.description:
Has the crypto market hit bottom yet, or will the sell-off continue?

Seeking Alpha analysts Jack Bowman, Mike Fay and Mandela Amoussou gave us their thoughts on the current state of the crypto market. 

Jack Bowman: I think it really depends on what kind of crypto we're talking about. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is looking at fundamental, structural support, given that $94,000 is around the price of mining a Bitcoin currently. That's a good indicator of a bottom, as it will become more profitable for mining operations to buy Bitcoin than mine it below these levels. So I can imagine that there's going to be a lot of buying pressure at these levels to keep Bitcoin from going very far below this figure. 

I'm not a Bitcoin maximalist per se, but I just don't see the same structural bullishness and institutional demand for altcoins. Even with ETFs and treasury companies buying up Ethereum (ETH-USD) and Solana (SOL-USD), it still might not be enough to buoy the price if investors continue to "sell risk" and dump assets like cryptocurrencies. 

So if it's not a bottom, and I'm wrong, then altcoins will get slaughtered, and I'm not interested in taking on that kind of risk. 

For me, I'm not buying any crypto right now, but I'm not selling my Bitcoin allocation, either.

Mike Fay: My read on the crypto market right now is that we have not bottomed yet. However, I suspect there are some key levels on some of the larger market cap coins that could start to bring in bids. I think $120 is an important level on Solana (SOL-USD). If Ethereum (ETH-USD) breaks $2,500 on a weekly close, I'd be very surprised. Finally, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) should stabilize somewhere between $80k and $82k, in my view.

Mandela Amoussou: At this point, I think it will be hard to tell what the actual floor will be for Bitcoin and the rest of crypto because the typical four-year cycle, which was the main benchmark for us analysts, has been invalidated, and retail participation, which drove previous cycle rallies, is no longer the dominant crypto market driver since the spot ETFs and crypto treasury strategies went mainstream.

A recalibration of market sentiment on the macro level will determine if crypto is close to bottoming. For now, I think it is not the bottom yet for Bitcoin and crypto. With the US Dollar Index (DXY) continuing to climb, risk‑beta assets like crypto are likely to bleed more for a while.

data.title: Big Tree Cloud receives Nasdaq notice regarding minimum bid price deficiency
data.description:
• Big Tree Cloud (DSY) announced on Friday that it has received a written notification from Nasdaq, notifying the company that it is not in compliance with Nasdaq's requirement to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share.
  • The notification has no effect on the company's Nasdaq listing status. 
  • The company has been provided a compliance period of 180 calendar days, or until May 18, 2026, to regain compliance. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Darkiris announces receipt of Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• DarkIris (NASDAQ:DKI) announced on Friday that on November 18, 2025, it received a deficiency letter  from the Nasdaq.
  • The notice mentioned that the company, based upon the closing bid price of Class A Ordinary Shares over the 30 consecutive business days, is not in compliance with the requirement to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share of its shares for continued listing on Nasdaq, as set forth in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • If at any time before May 18, 2026, the closing bid price of the shares reaches or exceeds $1.00 per share for a minimum of 10 consecutive business days, the staff will provide written notification that the company has satisfied the compliance.
